response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned full-stack and systems software developer with over 15 years of experience, but his profile has virtually no overlap with the Principal Performance Architect role. The position requires deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware, SIMD/MIMD architectures, and deep neural network optimization — none of which appear anywhere in his resume. His low-latency DLang work at Dunnhumby and recent AI-adjacent tooling at re:cinq are peripheral at best, and his Bachelor's-only education falls well short of the preferred advanced degrees for a principal-level hardware role.
strengths: 15+ years of professional engineering experience across diverse domains including low-latency services, embedded systems, and full-stack development.
Demonstrated performance-conscious software engineering using async I/O, caching, and high-throughput micro-services architectures at Dunnhumby.
Recent exposure to AI-agent infrastructure and agentic workflows at re:cinq, showing interest in the AI domain.
concerns: Zero experience with SoC or IP modeling — the primary technical requirement of the role.
No GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or deep neural network training/inference optimization background whatsoever.
Only a Bachelor's degree in Computer Science (2012), significantly below the PhD or Master's + extensive hardware experience expected at the principal level for this domain.

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware architecture experience
No AI accelerator, GPU, or DNN optimization expertise
Broad software engineering tenure and low-latency background
